basic_info.name_zh: 科学技术联合大学院大学
basic_info.name_en: Korea University of Science and Technology
aliases: Korea University of Science and Technology
Korean University of Science and Technology

key_people.name: DaeIm Kang (강대임)
key_people.title: President
key_people.title_description: 6th President of UST, overseeing all academic and administrative operations across 30 national research institute campuses
key_people.description: Former President of Korea Research Institute of Standards and Science (KRISS), former Vice President of DGIST, KAIST PhD in Mechanical Engineering. Appointed Feb 2025 for a 4-year term.

departments.name: KAERI Campus (Korea Atomic Energy Research Institute)
departments.description: Offers advanced nuclear system engineering, quantum energy chemical engineering, radiation measurement and detection programs

departments.name: ETRI Campus (Electronics and Telecommunications Research Institute)
departments.description: Offers broadband network technology, information security engineering, mobile communication, computer software engineering programs

departments.name: KIST Campus (Korea Institute of Science and Technology)
departments.description: Offers biomolecular science, nano surface technology, neuroscience programs

departments.name: KARI Campus (Korea Aerospace Research Institute)
departments.description: Offers aircraft system engineering, space launch vehicle system engineering, satellite systems and applications engineering programs

departments.name: IBS Campus (Institute for Basic Science)
departments.description: Offers nuclear fusion and other frontier basic science programs

departments.name: KRIBB Campus (Korea Research Institute of Bioscience and Biotechnology)
departments.description: Offers functional genomics, bio-analytical science programs

departments.name: KRICT Campus (Korea Research Institute of Chemical Technology)
departments.description: Offers green chemistry and environmental biotechnology programs

departments.name: KIMM Campus (Korea Institute of Machinery and Materials)
departments.description: Offers manufacturing system engineering, nano-mechatronics programs

departments.name: AX Strategy Center
departments.head: Park Seung-nam
departments.description: Established May 1, 2026, dedicated to driving AI transformation (AX) across all academic and administrative operations, starting from AY2027

recent_events.title: UST establishes AX Strategy Center in organizational restructuring
recent_events.description: UST carried out an organizational restructuring effective May 1, 2026, creating the new AX Strategy Center to drive AI transformation across academic and administrative operations. Park Seung-nam was appointed as center director.
recent_events.impact: Signals UST's strategic commitment to integrating AI into graduate education across all 30 national research institute campuses

recent_events.title: 45 UST members receive government awards on Science and ICT Day 2026
recent_events.description: 45 UST faculty members and students received government awards at the 2026 Science, Information and Communications Day ceremony, demonstrating the institution's research excellence.

recent_events.title: UST holds 2026 spring degree conferral ceremony with 202 graduates
recent_events.description: UST conferred degrees to 202 master's and doctoral graduates at the spring 2026 commencement ceremony, described as value-creating global talents.

recent_events.title: UST appoints 165 new faculty including world top 0.1% highly cited researchers
recent_events.description: UST appointed 165 new faculty members across 24 national research institute campuses, including Highly Cited Researchers (HCR) and global TOP strategy research group leaders. Over half are under 40 years old.

recent_events.title: UST has 11 projects selected for 2025 Top 100 National R&D Excellence, most among Korean universities
recent_events.description: 11 research projects by UST faculty were selected for the '2025 Top 100 National R&D Excellence' by the Ministry of Science and ICT, the highest number among all Korean universities.

recent_events.title: DaeIm Kang inaugurated as 6th President of UST
recent_events.description: Former KRISS President Dr. DaeIm Kang was inaugurated as the 6th President of UST for a 4-year term, outlining a 100-year vision focused on cultivating value-creating global S&T talents.

related_entities.org_description: Direct supervisory ministry responsible for UST's establishment, approval, and administrative guidance

related_entities.org_name: Korea Institute of Science and Technology
related_entities.org_description: KIST is one of the 30 consortium research institutes serving as UST campuses for graduate education
ETRI is one of the 30 consortium research institutes; UST headquarters is located opposite ETRI in Daejeon
IBS is one of the 30 consortium research institutes offering frontier basic science programs including nuclear fusion

related_entities.org_name: Korea Research Institute of Bioscience and Biotechnology
related_entities.org_description: KRIBB is one of the 30 consortium research institutes offering functional genomics and bio-analytical science programs
Ministry of Education granted UST its establishment authorization in October 2003

core_business: UST is Korea's only national consortium graduate university, jointly operated by 30 government-funded research institutes under the Ministry of Science and ICT. It offers exclusively master's and doctoral programs across 44 interdisciplinary majors (49 sub-majors) in national strategic fields including IT, BT, NT, ET, and ST. The university employs innovative teaching methods including lab rotation, 1:1 apprenticeship education, and on-site research. With approximately 1,600 faculty drawn from 8,000+ PhD-level researchers at participating institutes, UST maintains a student-to-faculty ratio of 0.94:1. All students receive monthly research stipends (minimum 1.43M KRW for master's, 1.9M KRW for doctoral). As of March 2026, there are approximately 1,583 enrolled students (32% international) and 3,985 cumulative graduates. Doctoral graduates average over 4 SCI publications per person.

collection_meta.notes: Enriched profile from Korean/English/Chinese Wikipedia, official website, and 2025-2026 news coverage
